executive_summary: Build failure analysis for attempt 3 reveals a critical configuration error. The GitHub Actions workflow file (.github/workflows/build.yml) is not found or not accessible in the repository. This prevents the build workflow from being triggered, resulting in a 404 HTTP error from the GitHub API workflow dispatch endpoint. This is a blocking issue that must be resolved before any build can proceed.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Workflow File Not Found
errors.description: The GitHub Actions workflow dispatch endpoint returned a 404 Not Found error when attempting to trigger the build workflow. The workflow file at path .github/workflows/build.yml does not exist in the repository or is not accessible with the current permissions.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches response_body={
errors.root_cause: The workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml does not exist in the repository at branch 'main', or the GitHub token being used lacks the necessary permissions (workflow/actions scope) to access or dispatch the workflow. Additionally, GitHub Actions may be disabled for this repository.
errors.impact: Complete build pipeline failure. No builds can be triggered or executed until the workflow file is created or permissions are corrected. This blocks all CI/CD operations including compilation, testing, and deployment.

automated_steps: 1. Verify GitHub Actions is enabled for the repository in Settings > Actions > General
2. Create the missing workflow file at .github/workflows/build.yml with proper Maven build configuration
3. Ensure the GitHub token has 'workflow' and 'actions:write' permissions
4. Verify the workflow file is committed to the 'main' branch
5. Test workflow dispatch manually from GitHub Actions UI
manual_verification: Check repository settings to confirm Actions are enabled
Review token permissions in GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens
Verify .github/workflows directory exists and contains build.yml
Confirm workflow syntax is valid using GitHub Actions validator
recommended_fix.compliance_notes: Workflow file creation and token permission changes require approval from repository administrators. All changes must be logged in the audit trail with justification for compliance purposes.

audit_log.analysis_method: Automated log parsing and error classification
audit_log.compliance_status: Analysis completed per enterprise DevOps standards
audit_log.next_steps: Escalate to repository administrators for workflow file creation and permission review
